component.name: Button
component.description: Triggers an action or navigation; the primary lever for user intent in editorial workflows.
component.note: Both sizes use body-m (14px/20px); only min-height differs. Synced to Figma '❖ Button'.

useCases: Cancel or secondary action alongside primary (neutral)
Inline low-emphasis control where a filled button is too heavy (text)
Toggle / segmented control where one option stays active (selected)

commonPatterns.name: Page header primary action
commonPatterns.description: Single primary button pinned to the right of the 726px header, paired with a neutral or text button for Cancel.

commonPatterns.name: Destructive confirm
commonPatterns.description: Danger button inside a confirmation modal. Always requires a neutral Cancel alongside it.

commonPatterns.name: Inline row action
commonPatterns.description: Text variant inside a table row — no fill, no border, minimal visual weight until hover.

commonPatterns.name: Dropdown / menu trigger
commonPatterns.description: Label with a trailing chevron (iconRight) signals an expandable menu.

commonPatterns.name: Toggle in a segmented control
commonPatterns.description: selected renders the forest tint and exposes aria-pressed; pair with onClick to flip state.

antiPatterns.scenario: Two primary buttons on the same view
antiPatterns.reason: Breaks the one forest.500 moment per view rule — both compete for the primary action.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep one primary; demote secondary actions to neutral or text variant.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using primary for navigation (e.g. 'Go to article')
antiPatterns.reason: Navigation is not a primary action — it doesn't commit or mutate state.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a text button or a link component.

antiPatterns.scenario: Danger button without a confirmation step
antiPatterns.reason: Destructive actions are irreversible — users must confirm intent.
antiPatterns.alternative: Open a confirmation modal or inline confirm/cancel pair before executing.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using selected for a one-shot action button
antiPatterns.reason: selected (aria-pressed) communicates a persistent toggle state, not a momentary click.
antiPatterns.alternative: Leave selected off; only use it for toggle/segmented controls that retain state.

slots.label: Required — sentence-case text (omit only for icon-only, then set aria-label)
slots.iconRight: Optional — 16px icon after label (Figma: iconAfter; commonly a chevron)

interactions.hover: primary → forest.700 fill; neutral/text → forest.100 tint + forest.500 border + forest.700 text; danger → danger.800
interactions.press: primary → forest.900; neutral/text → forest.200 tint; danger → danger.900
interactions.selected: forest.50 fill + forest.700 border + forest.700 text across all appearances; sets aria-pressed
interactions.disabled: neutral.100 fill (transparent for text), neutral.400 text, cursor not-allowed
interactions.loading: Label hidden via opacity (stays in a11y tree); spinner overlays; aria-busy set

accessibility.keyboardSupport: Enter and Space activate; Tab moves focus in/out
accessibility.screenReader: Icon-only buttons must carry aria-label; loading announces via aria-busy; selected announces via aria-pressed
accessibility.contrastNote: Danger fill uses danger.700 (white text 5.81:1) rather than Figma's danger.500 (3.54:1, fails AA).

figmaDivergences.reason: Figma's danger.500 + white text is 3.54:1, failing WCAG AA. danger.700 is 5.81:1.

aiHints.context: Reach for Button whenever the user needs to trigger an action. Default to neutral variant when a primary already exists on the view. Use text variant inside list rows. Use selected only for toggle/segmented controls that retain state.
neverUseWhen: The action is navigation-only — use a link or text button styled as a link
The surface already has a primary button — use neutral or text instead
The label would exceed ~4 words — rethink the label before widening the button
You need a momentary click — don't set selected, which implies persistent toggle state
